diagrams.description: This timeline traces the evolution of Commercial Law from medieval merchant customs to modern international frameworks. Key milestones include the Lex Mercatoria (medieval merchant law), the English Sale of Goods Act 1893, the U.S. Uniform Sales Act 1906, the Uniform Commercial Code (UCC) drafted in 1952, the UN Convention on Contracts for the International Sale of Goods (CISG) in 1980, and the UNIDROIT Principles of International Commercial Contracts (1994). The timeline also notes the adoption of national commercial codes in civil law countries during the 19th century and contemporary developments in digital commerce and smart contracts.
